plans.title: 音频编排边界收敛与 Orchestrator 计划化修复

notes.text: Started P0-A/P0-B: contract-first audio inputs and plan-first activation path
Completed P0-A/P0-B: contract-first audio input + workflow.plan activation path; targeted tests passed (12+4+3)
Refined execution plan to S1-S5 with Plan-and-Execute + adaptive replanning terminology
Completed S3/S4 cleanup: removed strategy/mode-driven routing from orchestrator plan path and tool global policy reads; updated unit tests; full pytest blocked by missing sqlalchemy
Validated S3/S4 changes with targeted regressions: 19 passed (audio orchestration + video audio route injection)
Implemented S5 adaptive replanning loop (evaluate/decide/apply, standby activation, abort on budget exhaustion, trace persistence); targeted tests now 22 passed
All planned work delivered and validated (22 passed); awaiting explicit user acceptance

plans.title: 编排去规则化收口与边界清理

notes.text: Start implementation: execute S1/S2 de-rule refactor in orchestrator
Completed S1-S4 refactor in orchestrator; S5 pending full pytest due missing deps (sqlalchemy/pydantic)
S1-S5 implemented and targeted verification passed (24 passed). Awaiting user confirmation before completion transition
Cleanup pass done: removed unused legacy helper methods and deprecated legacy env vars docs; targeted tests still 24 passed
Implementation and validation done; waiting for final user confirmation

plans.title: MAS 编排边界解耦与单 Episode Runtime Kernel 重构

notes.text: Drafted overall refactor plan with phased roadmap: boundary freeze, runtime kernel, gate extraction, leaf-agent cleanup, quick HITL integration, and project reuse
Started Phase 0: created audit checklist and froze banned-pattern review as the first implementation milestone
Added an architecture review gate to reject faux de-ruling refactors that only move global switches into internal variables without changing ownership boundaries
Completed P0-2 runtime/control-plane detailed design: clarified workflow_session, node, attempt, gate, decision models and the shared single-episode runtime kernel boundary
Completed P0-4 contract design: defined ExecutionIntent, ExecutionContract, GateResult, and their ownership boundaries to replace implicit orchestration protocols
Completed P0-5 initial Phase 1 cut: introduced workflow runtime ORM tables, runtime session service, runtime kernel skeleton, and rerouted task/task_queue ownership through the kernel boundary
Focused runtime unit tests could not run locally because sqlalchemy is missing from the current environment; py_compile verification passed for changed files
Completed P0-6 script gate minimal loop: runtime kernel now pauses at script review, records approve/revise/replan decisions, and resumes through the single-episode kernel
Validated P0-6 in the backend uv environment after syncing the test extra: py_compile passed and targeted runtime tests passed (4 passed)

Post-P0-6 architecture review found two remaining boundary drifts: revise/replan decisions do not yet materially affect the next script attempt, and the runtime kernel still calls orchestrator private helpers for stage execution
Adjusted the next milestone ordering by inserting P1-0 before evaluator extraction to harden script gate decision contracts and shrink kernel-to-orchestrator private coupling
Completed P1-0: script revise/replan now flow through explicit runtime contracts and a stage-level script runner instead of implicit episode_context coupling
Validated P1-0 in the backend uv environment: py_compile passed for changed files and focused runtime tests passed again (4 passed)
Started P1-1 with the first evaluator extraction slice: moved workflow-level video audio probing and normalized delivery-gate results into a dedicated audio delivery evaluator service
Validated the first P1-1 slice in the backend uv environment: py_compile passed and targeted orchestrator/runtime tests passed (17 passed, 4 skipped)
Completed the core P1-2 slice: video_generator now consumes an explicit execution contract, no longer reads workflow.plan/audio_route, and no longer patches tool calls before execution
Validated the P1-2 slice in the backend uv environment: py_compile passed and targeted video_generator/orchestrator/runtime tests passed (23 passed, 4 skipped)

Advanced P1-1b without creating a new rule center: audio agent participation now reads plan/policy state only, workflow-level audio gate observation is captured outside _should_run_agent, and BGM mix runtime checks moved behind evaluator outputs
Validated the P1-1b slice in the backend uv environment: py_compile passed and targeted audio/orchestrator/video_generator/runtime tests passed (24 passed, 4 skipped)

Refined P1-1b after plan audit: no goal drift detected; narrowed the remaining work to orchestrator-heavy audio preflight/standby logic and added a guardrail that new route/contract seams must remain declarative-only

Completed P1-1b by moving the remaining audio preflight and standby activation rules into a dedicated audio orchestration policy service so orchestrator now consumes policy outputs instead of hosting those rule branches directly
Validated the completed P1-1b slice in the backend uv environment: py_compile passed and targeted audio/orchestrator/video_generator/runtime tests passed again (24 passed, 4 skipped)

Refined P1-3 before execution: clarified that video_composer must move to an explicit execution boundary, stop inferring mix mode from add_bgm/add_voiceover/static_context.requests on the live path, and treat those fields as compatibility-only inputs

Synchronized downstream phases after terminology/boundary review: updated P1-4 and P2 to require explicit execution boundaries for compose and scene-level gates, clarified project reuse as the same hybrid subagents boundary model, and corrected the plan opening to reflect that video_generator was already fixed in P1-2

Aligned the plan with the midpoint architecture minutes: kept hybrid subagents as the total architecture label, treated policy/gate orchestration and contract-bounded execution as implementation-boundary descriptions only, and tightened P1-3 wording accordingly

Tightened the refactor plan so temporary compatibility paths must be retired in later phases; added explicit compatibility retirement work for composer legacy flags and runtime workflow.plan/audio_route seams

Completed P1-3: video_composer now resolves primary mix mode from an explicit execution boundary, orchestrator injects and normalizes the composer execution boundary, and legacy flags are reduced to a single compatibility adapter with fail-fast conflict diagnostics
Validated the P1-3 slice in the backend uv environment: py_compile passed and targeted composer/video_generator/orchestrator/runtime tests passed (31 passed, 4 skipped)

Progress audit confirmed no objective drift after P1-3: completed slices still match the hybrid-subagents target, and the main remaining transition seam is quick mode one-shot UI/runtime integration rather than backend architecture rollback
Refined P1-4 into backend API, frontend state, workspace UI, transport integration, and focused validation slices to prevent quick mode work from reintroducing one-shot assumptions

Completed P1-4a/P1-4b/P1-4c/P1-4d: quick mode now carries runtime session/gate state in the frontend store, exposes runtime and script-decision APIs through the client, renders a script-gate workspace instead of the old one-shot processing panel, and refreshes runtime state via polling plus websocket-triggered syncs

Partial P1-4 validation completed: backend runtime py_compile passed and runtime session tests passed (4 passed); frontend full type-check remains blocked by repository-wide pre-existing issues in __tests__/utils/test-helpers.ts and tsconfig.tsbuildinfo writes, so P1-4e remains open

Scope realignment discussion removed heavy HITL expansion from the current refactor scope: frontend work is capped at minimal quick-mode adaptation plus P1-4e verification, and compatibility retirement becomes the next mainline after P1-4e
Reordered downstream planning: current scope continues with P2-4/P2-5 seam retirement, while storyboard/scene-video gates and project reuse move to next-scope items

Completed the first compatibility-retirement slice: composer plan actions now emit explicit compose_mode, video_composer rejects legacy live inputs without an execution boundary, and runtime_kernel no longer injects workflow.plan/audio_route into orchestrator resume input

Validated the compatibility-retirement slice in the backend uv environment: py_compile passed and targeted composer/audio/runtime tests passed (26 passed, 4 skipped); frontend P1-4e smoke remains open

Completed P1-4e and closed the minimal frontend adaptation scope: websocket completion now follows the same result-overlay path as polling, and a no-incremental frontend type-check surfaced only the pre-existing __tests__/utils/test-helpers.ts errors

Post-retirement architecture review found that the current scope cannot close yet: orchestrator still owns tail-stage runtime execution, live execution-boundary assembly, and audio-specific controller logic, so the plan was extended with an orchestrator ownership closure phase
Frontend scope remains frozen at minimal adaptation; storyboard/scene-video gates and project reuse stay deferred until the new orchestrator ownership closure work is complete

Refined the current-scope closure into an explicit _execute_impl ownership subplan covering tail-stage runtime ownership, execution-boundary live assembly, adaptive controller residuals, and _build_persistence_payload migration

Tightened the current-scope acceptance to require migrating _build_workflow_plan, _build_activation_pool, _resolve_agent_runtime_overrides, and _build_audio_contract out of orchestrator instead of leaving them as transitional seams

Added a current-scope completion gate: final closure now requires explicit review of delivery purpose, target design boundaries, and the migrated code itself instead of inferring completion from phase progress

Completed the first P2-6a ownership slice: runtime kernel now executes storyboard/image and scene_video/video through a dedicated post-script stage runner, persists node attempts/status, and only the remaining compose/quality tail still falls through to orchestrator
Validated the first P2-6a slice in the backend uv environment: py_compile passed and focused runtime tests passed again (4 passed)

Completed the first P2-6b ownership slice: execution-boundary live assembly now resides in ExecutionBoundaryAssembler, and orchestrator no longer owns _build_agent_execution_contract, _apply_execution_boundary_to_agent_input, or _resolve_agent_runtime_overrides
Validated the combined P2-6a/P2-6b slices in the backend uv environment: py_compile passed and focused runtime/video/composer/audio gate tests passed (32 passed, 4 skipped)

Tightened current-scope planning again after anti-llm-decision review: domain-specific strategy files must not directly generate active_pool/standby_pool/actions/decision_reason; build_audio_preflight_plan and apply_audio_standby_activation are now explicit removal-or-downgrade targets in the current scope

Completed the P2-6f anti-llm-decision slice: live orchestration no longer imports or consumes audio_orchestration_policy plan builders, execution-boundary overrides now resolve from workflow.task_specs instead of workflow.plan.actions, and focused backend uv validation still passes (32 passed, 4 skipped)

Completed the remaining current-scope ownership closure: orchestration context persistence moved into OrchestrationStateAdapter, runtime replan switched from hard-coded audio rules to structured LLM decisions with gate facts, completion projection moved to WorkflowCompletionAdapter, and focused backend uv validation passed (30 passed, 4 skipped)
Architecture review reopened Phase 6: _execute_impl still keeps gate-inline-polling and inline runtime replan, and the orchestrator/subagent/control-plane master-worker protocol is not yet explicit
Extended the current-scope plan with P2-11 through P2-15 to converge orchestration logic, gating logic, and the master-worker communication protocol before allowing closure

Partially completed P2-11 through P2-14: introduced an OrchestrationProtocol host, switched runtime decisions to consume report plus gate_events, and removed the old inline audio gate->runtime_replan chain; remaining work is to move gate ownership and decision apply fully into control-plane and migrate decision_basis/route payload observability out of orchestration state

Observability review confirmed the project already has monitoring, event, and websocket infrastructure; current-scope work should migrate orchestration trace out of orchestration state into a dedicated observation/trace adapter rather than expanding monitoring_service or websocket responsibilities

Continued Phase 6 protocol/governance cleanup: OrchestrationProtocol now only owns master-worker message envelopes, OrchestrationRuntimeController triggers boundary gates from subagent reports, orchestration trace moved into OrchestrationObservationAdapter, and focused backend uv validation passed (36 passed, 4 skipped)

Tightened the current-scope closure gate after QA review: if obvious policy-first orchestration or main-loop gate/replan pulling remains, Phase 6 cannot close and the plan cannot be archived; the next action is continued root-cause analysis rather than local seam acceptance

Checkpointed the plan instead of forcing closure: current scope retains real progress but still has unresolved orchestrator main-loop ownership, runtime controller, protocol-boundary, and end-to-end coverage issues, so follow-up work moves to a narrower successor plan

plans.title: Orchestrator 主循环 Ownership 收口与协议硬边界化

notes.text: Created a narrowed successor plan after checkpointing PLAN-20260308-003; focus is now limited to orchestrator main-loop ownership, runtime controller purity, protocol hard boundaries, and real end-to-end coverage

Plan scope is complete on the MAS contract-hardening line, but final closeout is blocked by follow-up runtime-kernel regressions in task_queue/runtime_kernel/tasks API; the blocking work is tracked in PLAN-20260311-005

plans.title: Runtime Kernel Regression Closeout 与调度安全修复

notes.text: Created a dedicated hotfix follow-up for runtime-kernel regressions: audio-capable task routing safety, stage-success completion semantics, and API in-process runner gating
Hotfix implementation is no longer the main blocker; final closeout is blocked by single-episode mainline unification and runtime-kernel migration work tracked in PLAN-20260311-006

plans.title: Single-Episode 主线冻结与 Runtime Kernel 迁移收口

notes.text: Created a new managed plan to freeze OrchestratorAgent as the single-episode MAS mainline, migrate EpisodeRuntimeKernel back into control-layer responsibilities, and freeze state/memory boundaries without expanding into a full memory-system refactor

Started U0/U1: mainline governance is frozen to OrchestratorAgent and a code-backed keep/delete matrix now classifies which EpisodeRuntimeKernel responsibilities must be retained as control-layer substrate versus migrated out of the kernel

Closed after Stage G retired workflow_order from production orchestration, externalized selection/decomposition prompts, removed stale test references, and added execute_impl-level wiring proof with focused verification passing

plans.title: Frontend Runtime/Gate 最小同步修复

notes.text: Created minimal frontend repair plan to make quick-runtime nodes and script gate observable/actionable from quickRuntime without broad UI refactor

plans.title: API/Worker 日志治理统一与可观测性收口

notes.text: Drafted logging governance plan: unify API/Celery logging ownership, remove duplicate access logging, and keep MAS logic isolated from observability changes

Started implementation: introduced centralized dictConfig owner, removed main.py basicConfig and duplicate request access middleware, and switched Celery/task_queue off legacy configure_mas_logging wiring

plans.title: Concept Planner 超时治理与 Provider Deadline 收口

notes.text: Drafted timeout-governance plan to replace concept_planner equal-split budgeting with stage budgets and shared-deadline provider fallback, based on proxy/direct latency probes and recent timeout logs

Implemented the first timeout-governance slice: removed equal-split budgeting from concept_planner, introduced stage budgets/floors, switched Zhipu fallback to shared-deadline semantics, and passed focused unit tests (7 passed)

plans.title: Orchestrator Decomposition Boundary Refactor

notes.text: Drafted decomposition-boundary refactor plan to preserve two-step llm-centric planning while separating selection rationale, assignment contract, and subagent local planning

Implemented the first boundary-refactor slice: narrowed decomposition to assignment contract fields, exposed task_assignment in plan context, aligned runtime_hints consumption, and validated with focused tests plus a real planning probe where decomposition dropped from prior timeout behavior to a 13s successful response

Completed broader regression for the first slice: full affected unit suites passed (58+6+9), planning probe remained successful, and production-code audit confirmed old decomposition fields no longer drive the live orchestration path

Refreshed closeout governance after 016/017/019 convergence: conditional_tasks is now documented as a bounded runtime-activation appendix rather than part of the core per-agent contract, ContextContractAssembler is recorded as the canonical host, and residual project-side runtime_overrides remains compatibility debt rather than a repo-wide closeout blocker

Synchronized the closeout governance state: 010 is now acceptance-ready from an architecture-review perspective with no further implementation round required, while _schedule_project_plan(...) remains a separate project-level retained-scope question rather than being pulled back into this plan

User confirmed closeout; plan completed. Final judgment keeps conditional_tasks as a bounded activation appendix, records runtime_overrides as post-010 residual compatibility debt, and leaves _schedule_project_plan(...) outside the decomposition-boundary scope

plans.title: Quick Mode HITL Run Ownership & Frontend Sync Fix

notes.text: Created a focused plan to repair quick-mode frontend/backend sync after repeated task submissions caused the UI to watch a different task than the one actually executing

Corrected the plan around HITL semantics: page entry should discover unfinished runs and let the user continue or create a new run, while submit should replace the old unfinished run instead of silently reusing it

Implemented the first execution slice: quick workspace session identity, unfinished-run discovery API, frontend continue/new-run prompt, submit-time replace-old-run path, task execution metadata persistence for celery_task_id, and focused backend/frontend verification

Extended the plan after diagnosing worker-side re-execution of terminal quick tasks from historical broker backlog; added a unified execution-eligibility policy and a consumer-side terminal short-circuit milestone

Implemented the shared task execution policy, applied it in both queueing and worker consumption, and added focused tests proving that terminal and waiting-gate quick runs are no longer re-executed from historical broker backlog

Extended the plan after diagnosing Celery backend crashes from malformed historical failure metadata; added a failure-result contract fix and stale-result cleanup milestone before the final quick HITL validation

Fixed Celery failure reporting to raise standard exceptions instead of writing malformed FAILURE meta, added focused celery_app regression tests, and validated the wrapper with targeted compile and pytest checks
Added a narrow dev-only helper script to reset the configured Celery broker/result Redis DBs; stale-result cleanup and post-reset HITL validation are still pending manual execution

Executed the dev-only Celery cleanup flow by stopping local API/worker/beat processes, flushing Redis DB 1/2 through the cleanup helper, and confirming no residual uvicorn/celery/watchmedo processes remain

Refined the plan after reproducing a frontend ownership bug: continue-existing-run itself does not create tasks, but the unfinished-run prompt is not a strong gate, so users can still fall through to form submit and unintentionally create a new run

Implemented the unfinished-run hard gate in VideoRequestForm: the creation form now stays locked until the user explicitly chooses continue-existing-run or create-new-run, and handleSubmit short-circuits while that prompt remains unresolved

Extended the plan after proving the current blocker is the dev harness rather than MAS mainline: resumed quick tasks are re-queued correctly, but Celery worker/beat were launched with undrained stdout/stderr pipes and the worker stalled in pipe_write, so the plan now includes a dedicated dev-runner output fix and resume-consumption verification milestone

Implemented R12: start_dev_uv.py now launches long-lived Celery worker/beat processes with inherited stdout/stderr by default, adds opt-in log forwarding via FOLLOW_CELERY_LOGS=1, and includes a focused launcher regression test plus compile/plan-index validation

Extended the plan after isolating a cross-run memory-boundary regression: cleanup_workflow() currently clears all workflow scopes including mas:<workflow_id>, so after script gate the shared MAS WM is rebuilt empty and image/video scene_info_ref payloads persist as empty objects; the next fix now targets shared-WM preservation across gate/resume boundaries

Implemented the shared-WM boundary fix: cleanup_workflow() now clears only agent-scoped memories while reset_workflow() remains the full reset path, and focused regressions now prove shared MAS facts survive gate cleanup and keep image/video scene-info payloads non-empty across runs

Superseded by PLAN-20260321-012 after the root cause was reclassified from tactical quick/HITL sync fixes to the broader queue-adapter vs runtime/control-plane boundary refactor; retained as historical evidence only

plans.title: Queue Adapter / MAS Runtime Boundary Refactor

notes.text: Created successor plan from the newer boundary diagnosis in SHO-20260321-0001: preserve PLAN-20260319-011 as the tactical predecessor, and promote queue-adapter vs runtime/control-plane boundary repair to the current main task
Clarified governance precedence: PLAN-20260319-011 is historical reference only; current task judgment follows SHO-20260321-0001 and the actual codebase state

Added a validated state-boundary summary to the successor plan: queue infra handles transport/scheduler semantics only, runtime/control-plane owns MAS execution state, and frontend current-run must consume runtime read models instead of queue semantics
Added explicit MAS/runtime vs backend queue decoupling guardrails to the successor plan so implementation and review reject any drift that reintroduces runtime semantics into queue or frontend layers
Added a minimal resume-semantics guardrail: continuing an existing run, resuming after a gate decision, and historical broker re-consumption must remain separate behaviors

Refined the successor plan into phased sub-tasks with explicit checkpoints so later slices cannot proceed before boundary freeze, backend extraction, frontend runtime-first convergence, and end-to-end validation pass

Tightened the successor plan as a current-delivery-stage plan only and expanded validation into a concrete checklist with focused backend/frontend suites, suggested commands, and manual checkpoint gates
Split the detailed delivery verification out of the main plan into a dedicated validation artifact so the plan stays focused on stage goals, checkpoints, and guardrails

Aligned the successor governance with the latest user decision: PLAN-20260319-011 is treated as a superseded predecessor and no longer appears in the primary resume path except as optional historical evidence

Completed Phase A planning artifacts: added the canonical boundary inventory, froze the runtime-first read-model priority and resume semantics separation, and marked Checkpoint A passed so Phase B may start from an explicit ownership baseline
Cleaned up successor-plan bookkeeping after Phase A validation: removed duplicate checklist markup, advanced the handoff focus to Phase B / S3, and resynced plan metadata

Completed Phase B / S3 minimal worker-host extraction: moved queued-run bootstrap and dispatch entry into a dedicated execution-host service, split lightweight mode resolution out of mode_router, and validated the slice with focused backend regressions (test_task_queue.py 6 passed, test_tasks_endpoint.py 5 passed)

Completed Phase B / S4 runtime ownership extraction: RuntimeSessionService now owns quick dispatch payload recovery and runtime failure writeback, task_queue.py no longer directly gets/creates runtime sessions or marks runtime failure state, and focused backend regressions passed (18 passed total), so Checkpoint B is now passed

Started Phase C validation closure: polling/websocket now preserve runtime-first failure/completion semantics, a focused step-0 failure integration case plus a narrow frontend runtime-check tsconfig were added, narrow tsc passed, and the remaining blocker is a targeted Jest runner timeout without assertion output

Further narrowed the Phase C frontend test surface by aligning runtime-gate-sync mocks with the same alias imports used by QuickModeWorkspace and stubbing heavy child panels; narrow tsc still passes, but the targeted single-case Jest path continues to time out locally, so the open blocker remains a runner-level validation gap

Field validation proved the queue/runtime fix now carries script approve->resume into media-agent entry; the newly exposed empty scene_info_ref/shared-WM continuity issue is explicitly treated as a separate external-window problem, so PLAN-20260321-012 remains focused on queue/runtime boundary closure and frontend runtime-first validation only

Closed the remaining current-window validation gap by extracting a shared runtime read-model helper, adding a standalone TypeScript validation script that passed explicit step-0 failure/awaiting_human/resume/completed assertions, and marking Phase C plus boundary-scoped Phase D complete while leaving shared-WM continuity in the other window
User confirmed formal closure: the original 012 scope is complete, all checkpoints remain passed, and later 013/014/015 work is treated as follow-on successor plans rather than unfinished 012 work

plans.title: Non-Artifact Stage Deliverables and Dispatch Convergence

notes.text: Created successor plan after the architecture discussion converged on two linked follow-up tasks: non-artifact stage deliverables must be formalized before dispatch convergence can safely remove per-dispatch memory composition

Froze the execution-order rule for the successor: analysis may proceed in parallel, but code changes are strictly serial with Task A (stage deliverables) required to pass before Task B (dispatch convergence) may enter implementation

Froze the architecture guardrails for the successor: shared WM remains live iterate-facts SoT, published stage deliverables are boundary snapshots rather than a parallel SoT, and dispatch is treated as a suspect historical abstraction rather than a preserved layer by default

Tightened the successor after plan review: froze the first implementation host decision as published deliverable record plus payload_ref, named the active-path memory-composition audit set explicitly, and added architecture-doc synchronization as part of dispatch-retirement acceptance

Started Task A implementation: added workflow published deliverables, script-gate publication, approve-time ref updates, and media readers that prefer published script deliverables; py_compile and focused deliverable checks passed, while the broader runtime-session pytest file still needs follow-up due harness-level timeout

Passed Task A checkpoint with focused backend pytest (11 passed) plus direct proofs for both with-HITL approve and wo-HITL published-deliverable reads; started Task B by removing mode_router memory composition, routing quick queued execution directly to OrchestratorAgent, and requiring explicit memory_services across active-path control-plane/helper adapters

Further shrank dispatch during Task B: dispatch_generation is now project-only and explicitly rejects quick mode, while direct proofs confirm quick queued execution bypasses dispatch entirely and project mode still flows through the remaining project helper path

Clarified the quick composition root during Task B: OrchestratorAgent no longer implicitly builds memory in __init__, quick live path now enters through explicit create_default(), and remaining script/post-script runner constructors were aligned to the same explicit-memory rule

Passed Task B checkpoint: quick mainline no longer uses dispatch or per-dispatch memory composition, helper adapters reject implicit memory rebuilds, dispatch is reduced to a project-only helper, and the architecture mapping now reflects that shrinkage

Superseded by PLAN-20260323-014 after post-implementation architecture review found residual drifts: published deliverable persistence still owned memory adapter/projection duties, media readers still fell back to live shared WM on missing deliverables, and project mode still retained implicit composition via mode_router.py plus EpisodeOrchestratorAgent

plans.title: Published Deliverable Boundary Correction and Dispatch Retirement

notes.text: Created successor correction plan after the post-013 architecture review converged on two serial follow-up tasks: published-deliverable boundary correction first, then project explicit composition and dispatch retirement

Froze the correction target state: shared WM remains the live iterate-facts SoT, published deliverables remain boundary outputs rather than a parallel SoT, and mode_router.py must be deleted rather than renamed or preserved as a thin wrapper

Completed Task A: PublishedDeliverableService now owns persistence/ref construction only, script-stage payload build and WM projection moved into a dedicated adapter, and downstream media readers no longer silently fall back to live shared WM when the published deliverable is missing

Completed Task B: project queued/direct paths now compose EpisodeOrchestratorAgent explicitly, AudioDeliveryGateEvaluator requires explicit memory_services, mode_router.py was deleted, and architecture docs were synchronized to the new no-dispatch project path

Superseded by PLAN-20260323-015 after follow-up review found two residual drifts outside the accepted 014 slice: image path still mixed live-WM and published-deliverable script-stage inputs, and PublishedDeliverableService still mutated session.input_payload directly

plans.title: Image Boundary Purity and Deliverable Runtime Ownership Repatriation

notes.text: Created successor correction plan after follow-up review confirmed two remaining drifts: image path still received live-WM and published-deliverable script-stage inputs in parallel, and PublishedDeliverableService still directly mutated runtime payload state
Froze the execution order for the successor: image path boundary purity first, deliverable runtime ownership repatriation second
Froze the target state: image path must consume a single script-stage truth source, and deliverable persistence must not own runtime/control-plane state mutation

Implemented Task A/B: removed IMAGE_GENERATOR live-WM script-stage pre-merge, moved published deliverable payload writeback out of PublishedDeliverableService and into orchestrator/runtime control-plane callers, and added focused regressions

Ran a broader backend regression on current-architecture-aligned unit/runtime suites (92 passed) and explicitly filtered out legacy singleton/WorkflowState/manual-harness integration tests from the acceptance slice
User confirmed closure; plan marked completed with scope held to the 015 acceptance slice only

plans.title: Canonical MAS Architecture Conformance and Compatibility Seam Retirement

notes.text: Created the true successor plan after 015 closure and canonical-alignment review confirmed the remaining work is no longer a narrow correction slice but a broader canonical MAS architecture conformance problem

Froze the residual grouping into four classes: explicit orchestrator cross-layer ownership, Context/Contract Assembler host absence, active compatibility seams, and secondary mainline/project semantic noise
Froze the serial execution order A -> B -> C -> D: clear explicit overreach first, then assembler convergence, then compatibility seam retirement, then secondary noise cleanup

Tightened harness-alignment guardrails: queue/standby/insertion artifacts must remain internal orchestrator-control-plane collaboration objects rather than leaf-facing contracts, and Phase A is limited to host/interface freeze plus explicit-overreach extraction while Phase B owns builder and contract convergence

Started Phase A implementation: extracted script-review boundary publish/projection out of OrchestratorAgent into ExecutionBoundaryAssembler plus RuntimeSessionService, relocated per-agent stage-input routing behind the assembler capability, and validated the slice with focused backend regressions (15 passed + 58 passed)

Froze the formal supporting-capability host to ContextContractAssembler in context_assembler.py, moved active orchestrator paths onto the new host/interface, demoted execution_boundary_assembler.py to a compatibility wrapper, and validated the host-freeze slice with focused regressions (87 passed) plus py_compile

Started the first Phase B builder-convergence slice by making downstream media and voice assembly prefer published script deliverable payloads over stale live WM facts, and validated the slice with focused regressions (89 passed) plus refreshed py_compile

Made runtime deliverable projection an explicit ContextContractAssembler capability so orchestrator startup no longer directly calls the projection adapter, and validated the slice with focused regressions (90 passed) plus refreshed py_compile

Formally closed Checkpoint A after re-auditing the active mainline: explicit orchestrator overreach for script-review boundary mixing and per-agent builder routing is gone, and the latest focused regression baseline (90 passed) remains the closure evidence

Advanced Phase B by moving published-deliverable resolution into explicit ContextContractAssembler receipts with required-vs-optional diagnostics, routing active media/image/video/voice builder assembly through assembler-supplied payload/views, and validating the slice with expanded focused regressions (98 passed)

Advanced the next Phase B/B3 ownership-correction slice by making ContextContractAssembler resolve published stage payloads from the canonical runtime carrier before shared-WM projection, preserving projection only as an explicit compatibility fallback, and validated the slice with focused regressions (101 passed) plus py_compile

Advanced the first Phase C read-side slice by rebuilding get_workflow_status from RuntimeSessionService runtime view instead of workflow.activation_pool compatibility data, and validated the slice with focused regressions (102 passed) plus py_compile

Advanced the next Phase C writer-side slice by moving OrchestrationStateAdapter compatibility writes off legacy workflow.plan / workflow.activation_pool / workflow.audio_route keys and into explicit workflow.diagnostics.compat snapshots, then validated the diagnostics quarantine with focused regressions (102 passed, 2 warnings) plus py_compile

Advanced the Phase C/C3 ownership slice by extracting execution queue / standby / insertion helpers out of OrchestrationStateAdapter into explicit OrchestrationQueuePolicy, then validated the ownership relocation with focused regressions (102 passed, 2 warnings) plus py_compile

Completed the Phase C mainline by converging human/system gate contract vocabulary in the runtime read model and closing Checkpoint C: legacy workflow.plan / workflow.activation_pool / workflow.audio_route keys are absent from app code, compatibility seams are diagnostics-only, and focused regressions still pass (102 passed, 2 warnings)

Refreshed the Phase D re-entry baseline: 017 is now the inherited SoT/projection guardrail, 019 is now the inherited authority-contract guardrail, 018 remains an independent blocked tooling plan, and the remaining active scope is Phase D secondary-mainline cleanup plus final closeout

Completed the first Phase D / D1 wrapper-thinning slice by removing the EpisodeOrchestrator project-foundation mini-host and delegating character-reference generation to the project service, then validated the slice with focused backend regressions (9 passed, 4 warnings); the remaining D2 inventory is now mainly dormant runners plus legacy orchestrator variants surviving in tests/testing_framework rather than active mainline callers

Completed the first D2 retirement slice by deleting the uncalled script_stage_runner.py and post_script_stage_runner.py modules after repo-wide inventory confirmed they no longer participate in backend/app or backend/tests; the remaining secondary-mainline noise is now concentrated in enhanced_orchestrator.py, react_orchestrator.py, and testing_framework.py

Froze the remaining D2 disposition before implementation: react_orchestrator.py, enhanced_orchestrator.py, and testing_framework.py will be retired from backend/app rather than isolated, because active app callers are absent and the surviving references are legacy tests/harness surfaces rather than canonical runtime paths

Completed the second D2 retirement slice by deleting react_orchestrator.py, enhanced_orchestrator.py, testing_framework.py, the leftover react_orchestrator prompt assets, and the legacy tests that only validated those alternate orchestrator semantics; backend/app and backend/tests now expose only the canonical orchestrator surface, while remaining mentions are limited to historical docs/plan records

Completed the Checkpoint D / final closeout audit: focused backend regressions protecting the inherited 017/019 guardrails passed (31 passed, 4 warnings), backend/app and backend/tests expose only the canonical mainline plus thin project wrapper, and the remaining legacy orchestrator mentions are confined to historical docs or explicitly annotated non-SoT references; 016 is now ready for acceptance discussion pending user confirmation

User confirmed the final closeout after the Checkpoint D audit and final regression evidence review; PLAN-20260323-016 is now formally completed, while PLAN-20260324-018 remains a separate blocked tooling plan outside this lifecycle

plans.title: SoT Clarity Audit and Projection Convergence

notes.text: Created a dedicated successor plan for the SoT clarity window after 016 Checkpoint C, explicitly separating facts SoT, runtime SoT, stage-boundary carrier, and agent-local trajectory instead of collapsing them into a single memory truth

Completed the first SoT/projection inventory and froze the business-question matrix plus residual ambiguity classes: wrong-object reads, transition bridges with boundary overreach, and summary surfaces mistaken for truth

Completed the convergence slice by retiring workflow_overview from active app code, constraining quick-mode reads to runtime-first plus coarse-task fallback, and renaming MAS summary/event payloads to facts-summary vocabulary

Retired active runtime-to-shared-WM boundary bridges: orchestrator no longer projects runtime carrier deliverables on startup, script-review boundary publish no longer writes shared-WM projected refs, and ContextContractAssembler now requires runtime-input published_deliverables instead of silently reading shared-WM projection
Validated the SoT convergence with backend py_compile, focused backend regressions (11 passed plus 24 passed), and a targeted frontend TypeScript check; the plan is now awaiting user confirmation

Reopened the plan after subagent QA found four blocking gaps: orchestrator boundary fail-open, mutable workflow_data boundary reads, quick-run discovery without runtime truth, and polling that could hide runtime-read failures behind coarse task status

Closed the blocking implementation round by making context assembly fail closed, pinning boundary reads to explicit runtime_input_payload, suppressing quick-run resume without runtime view, and restricting coarse polling fallback to explicit runtime-session bootstrap lag

Revalidated the blocking round with backend py_compile, focused backend regressions (84 passed, 2 warnings), and the targeted frontend TypeScript check; a new frontend integration regression test was added, but current Jest integration project config still fails before executing it because __tests__/setup.ts is not transformed as TypeScript

User confirmed closure; plan marked completed with SoT convergence accepted as done, while the remaining Jest integration-project configuration failure stays out of scope as a separate tooling follow-up

plans.title: Jest Integration Project Configuration Repair

notes.text: Created a separate small tooling follow-up after closing 017 so the Jest integration-project configuration failure can be repaired without reopening the SoT convergence scope

Execution started with a narrow acceptance boundary: repair only the integration-project boot/transform path enough to run the targeted runtime-authority regression, without broad frontend test-harness refactoring

Clarified that frontend does not own SoT/projection judgment; this plan only enforces consumer discipline against backend-declared authority boundaries, with any coarse fallback treated as controlled transitional behavior rather than frontend truth inference

Reclassified the plan to blocked after the initial syntax/config repair pass: the remaining targeted Jest failure is now attributed to the current WSL /mnt/d runner/filesystem environment rather than SoT-convergence or 019 contract work

Recorded the blocker semantics explicitly: blocked removes 018 from the main implementation critical path while keeping the tooling debt tracked; completion still requires the targeted Jest command to pass in a runnable environment, while future abandonment would require a superseded closeout rather than completed

plans.title: 前端消费权威边界审计与契约收口

notes.text: 在聚焦 inventory 之后创建独立 successor plan，因为问题已不再只是 Jest 或单一 quick hook，而是后端 contract 边界不够单一/显式，导致 frontend consumer 仍保留 fallback 和状态路由逻辑
明确与 017 和 018 分离：017 继续保持 SoT convergence 已关闭，018 继续保持 Jest/tooling 范围，019 专注 backend contract 收口和 frontend authority-routing 删除

结合 multi-agent discussion 细化计划：把 quick-mode 重新定性为 runtime endpoint 仍允许 non-authoritative absence state；补入 `/tasks/quick/current` 与 gate-decision response contract；拆分 project editorial/runtime 语义；把 same-object presentation mapping 明确列为非目标；并把执行顺序改成 backend-first contract 收口后再删除 frontend fallback

开始本窗口实现前分析并切到 in_progress：冻结 authority matrix，明确 backend-contract gap / frontend authority-routing / same-object presentation mapping 的边界，并锁定 backend-first 顺序为 quick runtime -> quick current -> gate decision -> project mode status/progress -> websocket terminal semantics

按冻结顺序完成 contract 收口与 frontend authority-routing 删除：quick runtime 改为 authoritative bootstrap/runtime 视图，quick current 收敛为 null|{task,runtime}，gate decision 必返 runtime，project mode 拆分 editorial/runtime 与 typed progress，websocket terminal 事件降级为 bounded summary + refresh trigger；验证记录见 PLAN-20260324-019-validation.md
Review 纠偏后确认计划仍未到验收态：主线收口已完成，但还存在一个 backend contract leak（runtime.approved_script 在未批准时向下游暴露）和一个 residual frontend fallback（project mode 把缺席 runtime 映射成 idle）；先修剩余边界，再回原窗口验收
完成 review 后补收口：runtime.approved_script 仅在 approve 路径写入，project response 为每个 episode 物化 runtime surface，project UI 不再把缺席 runtime 映射成 idle，并将 coarse /status 标注为 compatibility-only；现可回原窗口做 re-review
原窗口 re-review 发现新的 backend contract blocker：force_rerun 在 endpoint 预标记和 orchestrator 执行两侧都能绕过 editorial approval gate。该行为未被现有产品/UI/architecture 文档声明为显式 override contract，因此 019 重新回到 blocker-fix 优先状态
完成 force_rerun fail-close：endpoint 预标记现在先过 editorial approval gate，再决定是否 rerun；EpisodeOrchestrator 无条件跳过未批准 episode；新的 endpoint/orchestrator regressions 已写入 test_project_contracts.py 并通过
原窗口 re-review 确认未发现新的 blocker；019 的 backend contract 收口与 frontend authority-routing 删除已满足验收口径，剩余 TS/Jest/tooling 噪音继续归属 018 / 环境问题；用户确认 closeout，计划 completed

plans.title: single-episode 残余宿主统一与 legacy island 退役

notes.text: Created a managed successor plan after 016/017/019 closure to track the remaining single-episode residual cleanup: second execution host removal, legacy orchestration island retirement, and non-necessary residual helper/alias deletion

Froze the scope exclusion that keeps SeriesPlannerAgent and _schedule_project_plan(...) out of delete scope for now because they currently belong to project-level planning rather than single-episode runtime governance

Started implementation and completed the first retirement slice: removed backend/app/core/mas_* plus workflow_optimizer.py and the dedicated legacy MAS integration test after inventory confirmed they were no longer part of active app code

Completed the first project host-unification slice by routing project per-episode generation through TaskQueueService.queue_task instead of endpoint-local thread runners; project planning host remains explicitly retained outside this plan's delete scope

Completed the first residual-noise cleanup slice by deleting OrchestratorAgent.get_workflow_status() and its self-hosting test after grep proved no remaining callers; full-file pytest for test_orchestrator_runtime_mainline.py still timed out in the current environment, so acceptance relies on compile+grep evidence for this dead-code retirement

Completed the second residual-noise cleanup slice: queued host/orchestrator completion payloads now normalize on `status`, the worker-side `workflow_status` alias read is gone, frontend `enhanced_workflow_completed` / `enhanced_workflow_failed` aliases are removed, and the stale diagnostic script check for get_workflow_status() was deleted

Implementation and focused validation for the in-scope single-episode cleanup are complete; the plan now waits only for user confirmation while `_schedule_project_plan(...)` and SeriesPlannerAgent remain explicitly retained as project-level exclusions

User accepted closeout: PLAN-20260325-020 is completed with the single-episode residual cleanup finished; `SeriesPlannerAgent` and `_schedule_project_plan(...)` remain explicitly retained as project-level exclusions rather than unresolved single-episode architecture drift

plans.title: runtime_hints 收口与 runtime_overrides 残余兼容债务清理

notes.text: Created a dedicated successor plan after 010 closeout to clean up runtime_overrides as project-led but not project-only contract debt, without expanding 020 host/island cleanup scope

Froze the MAS-aligned migration direction: generic execution hints complete into runtime_hints, leaf agents stay on ExecutionContract, project-only options become typed wrapper-scoped inputs, and any compatibility path is limited to a single entry-boundary translator

Started execution by freezing the active inventory and implementation order: project request ingress, episode-wrapper passthrough/scatter injection, and generic assembler/orchestrator fallback are the remaining live runtime_overrides surfaces to remove in sequence

Completed the no-translator cleanup path: project entry now forbids the legacy runtime_overrides open bag in favor of typed wrapper options, wrapper passthrough/scatter injection is removed, and assembler/orchestrator canonical flow now terminates at runtime_hints -> ExecutionContract without reinjection

Focused validation passed for backend contract surfaces (24 passed + 65 passed); repo grep shows no runtime_overrides hits in backend/app or src, while full repo TypeScript still times out at 45s and remains non-blocking tooling noise

Follow-up cleanup switched the project endpoint to model_dump() and reran test_project_contracts.py successfully (8 passed), so the final typed-entry contract remains verified after the serializer modernization

User confirmed closeout; PLAN-20260325-021 is completed with canonical flow converged to typed project entry -> runtime_hints -> ExecutionContract -> leaf-agent, while the remaining apply_execution_boundary legacy-flag strip stays recorded as low-level compatibility debt rather than a blocker

plans.title: MAS canonical harness 回正与残余旁路清理总计划

notes.text: Created a managed successor plan to turn the current MAS architecture audit into a staged cleanup roadmap: active-path fail-close, bridge retirement, frontend authority cleanup, diagnostics hygiene, and a separate project-wrapper host decision

Froze the hard execution order and phase gates so this repo-level convergence can be advanced in verified slices without mixing single-episode harness cleanup, frontend authority work, and project-level wrapper host decisions

Tightened the managed plan against the current architecture baseline: supporting capability may still read canonical facts but may not self-heal missing boundary inputs, and project-wrapper host uniformity is now treated as a non-blocking tail decision rather than a blocker for single-episode harness convergence

Started execution after the plan-alignment pass: 022 is now in progress with the first Phase 1 slice landed, removing builder-side published-script self-resolution and the composer nested scene_outputs fallback; changed-file py_compile and focused boundary regressions passed (11 passed)

Completed the Phase 1 builder strictification slice: remaining direct integration callers now pass explicit script_stage_views, the integration probe passed (1 passed), and the next planned work moves to Phase 2 boundary-bridge and legacy-grammar retirement

Completed the Phase 2 bridge-retirement slice: removed ContextContractAssembler.project_runtime_payload_deliverables(...), deleted the retired ExecutionBoundaryAssembler alias, reduced published_deliverable_adapter.py to canonical payload building only, and validated the cleanup with changed-file py_compile, focused regressions (12 passed), and grep proving the bridge/alias symbols are gone

Completed the Phase 3 first-party authority cleanup slice: removed the frontend coarse /status binding and coarse response type, deleted the unused agent_progress direct handler/type path, and validated the changed runtime consumer files with a focused frontend type-check while the full repo tsc remains blocked only by the pre-existing __tests__/utils/test-helpers.ts syntax issue

Completed the Phase 4 diagnostics hygiene slice: moved audio observation writes into workflow.diagnostics.* keys, added focused workflow-completion tests that lock bounded_terminal_summary payloads to summary/refresh semantics, and validated the change with py_compile, focused regressions (3 passed, 58 deselected), and grep proving the old audio gate/signal keys are gone

Closed the Phase 5 tail decision without code changes: _schedule_project_plan(...) remains an explicit project-wrapper/product-job retained scope item, host uniformity is deferred rather than forced into single-episode harness cleanup, and the plan now waits for user confirmation after Phases 1-4 completed with focused validation

Governance sync reconciled the 022 markdown plan and validation ledger with the already-recorded completed lifecycle: the plan remains closed, no implementation phase was reopened, and successor cleanup continues separately in 023/024/025

plans.title: MAS residual tail cleanup 与 product-wrapper host 后续治理

notes.text: Created a managed successor after the post-022 audit showed the remaining issues are now repo-level residual tails rather than blockers for single-episode canonical harness convergence: first-party WS-derived telemetry state, coarse compatibility surface governance, and the product-wrapper host decision

Froze the successor scope so 023 does not reopen 022: blocker cleanup stays acceptance-ready in the earlier plan while residual cleanup proceeds as a separate governance stream with its own validation ledger

Moved the successor plan to in_progress and started Phase A on the three scoped frontend runtime-consumer files, with the change boundary frozen to removing WS-derived counters, keeping workflow_completed/workflow_failed as notification-plus-refresh triggers, and tightening reset so prior-run telemetry does not bleed into the next quick run

Completed Phase A first-party frontend runtime-read-model strictness: removed the event.state counter writers for concept_plan_ready/image_assets_ready/video_assets_ready, deleted the corresponding app-store fields and RealTimeProgress consumption, kept workflow_completed/workflow_failed as notification-plus-refresh triggers only, and validated the slice with a focused frontend type-check plus grep confirming the residual vocabulary is gone from src

Completed Phase B coarse compatibility surface governance by deleting the backend coarse /tasks/{id}/status endpoint, removing the remaining stale coarse-status test path, updating backend/API documentation to point at /{task_id}/runtime, refreshing the active deviation inventory, and validating the slice with changed-file py_compile, focused backend pytest (6 passed), and a zero-match grep across current code/docs while a supplemental frontend jest probe remained locally stuck without output

Closed Phase C with a successor decision instead of in-plan host implementation: _schedule_project_plan(...) remains explicit project-wrapper/product-job retained scope, the active deviation inventory now points to PLAN-20260325-024, and 023 moved to awaiting_user_confirmation after all three phases completed

Reopened the plan after review found one remaining MAS-misaligned frontend test fixture: __tests__/integration/runtime-gate-sync.test.tsx still mocked the deleted scenesPlanned/imagesGenerated/videosGenerated store fields, so a narrow cleanup slice is required before final closeout

Completed the stale frontend test-fixture cleanup slice by deleting the removed telemetry fields from __tests__/integration/runtime-gate-sync.test.tsx, re-running repo grep to confirm those fields are gone from src/__tests__/tests, and returning 023 to awaiting_user_confirmation while the targeted jest probe remained locally stuck without output

User accepted closeout after the stale fixture cleanup and deviation-inventory recheck; reran focused 023 validation (frontend runtime type-check, focused backend pytest for tasks endpoint and working-memory service, zero-match telemetry grep, and PLAN_INDEX.json parsing) and moved the plan to completed without reopening 022

plans.title: Project-wrapper host uniformity successor governance

notes.text: Created a managed successor after PLAN-20260325-023 Phase C decided that project-wrapper host uniformity should be governed separately from the residual single-episode cleanup stream

Froze the successor boundary so host work can converge only toward an independent product-job/shared host path or an explicit retained rationale, without being folded back into single-episode harness cleanup
User requested that 024 resume after the 026 checkpoint commit; lifecycle moved from draft to in_progress, with immediate focus limited to Phase A host inventory and ownership freeze

Completed Phase A host inventory and ownership freeze: the project planning path was confirmed to own only project-wrapper task/progress state, while single-episode runtime/control-plane ownership remains in the queued EpisodeOrchestratorAgent -> OrchestratorAgent path

Completed Phase B successor decision: rejected long-term retention of the endpoint-local planning host and selected convergence toward an independent product-job/shared host that stays outside the single-episode MAS runtime control plane

Refined the post-Phase-B execution path: split the former coarse Phase C into C1 project-state visibility plus host-neutral job-boundary work, followed by C2 minimal implementation or explicit defer, so the plan can adapt to real constraints without changing its architectural boundary

Completed Phase C1: confirmed that project read-model visibility is still process-local and current queue dispatch remains keyed by GenerationMode plus quick-runtime semantics, so C2 is now gated on explicit shared project-state visibility and a neutral job_kind/handler_key dispatch contract

Completed Phase C2 via explicit defer instead of direct migration: kept 024 bounded to host-uniformity governance, created PLAN-20260326-027 for the larger project-level read-model persistence and host-neutral job substrate work, and moved 024 to awaiting_user_confirmation
User confirmed closeout after the explicit-defer split; 024 is now completed without archive, and PLAN-20260326-027 remains the active successor for the larger project-level substrate work

plans.title: MAS residual cleanup successor for supporting-capability strictness and governance/test anchor convergence

notes.text: Created a managed successor cleanup plan after the handoff and architecture audit converged on the same baseline: 022/023 stay closed, 024 remains project-wrapper host-only, and the next window should handle residual cleanup rather than re-judge single-episode mainline correctness

Completed Phase 0 governance freeze by creating the successor plan plus validation ledger, freezing the phase order to supporting-capability fail-open -> compat event vocabulary -> governance asset consistency -> stale test/mock anchors, and adding an explicit user-confirmation gate between every phase

Completed Phase 1 supporting-capability fail-open closeout: removed load_scene_overview()'s legacy VideoMemoryAdapter fallback, added focused unit coverage locking helper and artifacts paths away from legacy adapter readback, and recorded that broader backend pytest stayed blocked in D state while changed-file py_compile, helper grep, and minimal module-level probes passed

Completed Phase 2 residual compat event and telemetry cleanup: removed the extra concept_plan_ready/image_assets_ready/video_assets_ready production publishes, dropped first-party agent-status-update/progress-update websocket handlers and message types, and validated the narrowed production vocabulary with backend py_compile, focused frontend type-check, and a zero-match grep across backend/app and src

Completed Phase 3 governance asset consistency repair: reconciled PLAN-20260325-022 markdown plus validation with the completed status already owned by PLAN_INDEX, refreshed the current-state pointer in PLAN-20260325-023, and validated the governance-only diff with PLAN_INDEX parsing and consistency review without reopening any lifecycle state

Completed Phase 4 stale test/mock anchor refresh: rewrote frontend websocket mocks and integration coverage to canonical event.progress/event.state plus current direct notices, tightened test setup so hook tests exercise the actual first-party websocket contract, and updated same-class backend websocket tests to the current broadcast surface while recording local jest/pytest D-state hangs as environment blockers

Completed the post-review Phase 4 follow-up: rewrote user-workflow browser smoke coverage to the current quick-workspace contract, fully rewrote backend websocket integration coverage to /api/v1/ws/connect plus current manager semantics, updated the websocket perf anchor to the current handshake path, and revalidated with clean grep plus backend py_compile and frontend type-check while focused jest/pytest remained blocked in D state

User confirmed final closeout; plan completed with residual cleanup accepted as done, while repeated local jest/pytest D-state hangs remain explicitly tracked as environment/tooling debt rather than an in-scope architecture or contract blocker

Recorded a post-closeout same-class micro cleanup without reopening lifecycle: removed the last three backend unit-test project_runtime_payload_deliverables stubs, verified zero remaining grep matches plus changed-file py_compile, and observed the same local focused-pytest D-state blocker already tracked as environment/tooling debt

plans.title: MAS residual purity successor for runtime read-path, scene-info contract, and governance inventory cleanup

notes.text: Created a managed successor after the latest handoff and direct codepath review confirmed the remaining issues are active residual purity problems rather than a reason to reopen 022/023/025 or fold 024 back into the single-episode cleanup stream

Completed Phase 0 governance freeze by creating the new successor plan plus validation ledger, freezing the phase order to runtime read-path purity, scene-info contract purity, low-level compat seam retirement, and architecture governance inventory refresh, with an explicit user-confirmation gate between every phase

Tightened the Phase 0 audit trail after review found the original diff note did not explicitly cover the two untracked governance files; recorded tracked PLAN_INDEX diff evidence, no-index added-file diffs for the new successor assets, and a focused git status snapshot so the governance-only change set is fully auditable

User confirmed Phase 0 and the plan moved to in_progress for Phase 1, with implementation explicitly limited to runtime read-path purity and focused validation that GET runtime reads no longer create sessions or repair default nodes

Completed Phase 1 runtime read-path purity: removed implicit bootstrap/repair from runtime-view builders, kept session creation/default-node initialization on explicit write-side owners, and validated the new no-write-on-read behavior plus endpoint absence/integrity contracts with backend py_compile, bootstrap-helper grep, and focused backend pytest (18 passed)

Completed Phase 2 scene-info contract purity: scene_info reference persistence now fails closed with explicit diagnostics, ContextContractAssembler no longer emits scene_info_payload fallback into leaf-facing static context, and focused backend pytest plus fallback-removal grep validated both success and fail-close paths

Synced the plan wording after Phase 2 so the status-management section no longer describes 026 as still in draft/Phase-0-only state; lifecycle remains in_progress while waiting for user confirmation before Phase 3

Completed Phase 3 low-level compat seam retirement: removed ContextContractAssembler.apply_execution_boundary(), stopped build_video_composer_context() from emitting legacy requests hints into active static context, moved legacy composer-vocabulary rejection to the explicit execution-contract boundary, and validated the change set with changed-file py_compile, production-only grep, and focused backend pytest (16 passed)

Completed Phase 4 architecture governance inventory refresh: updated mas_architecture_deviation_inventory_20260323.md to absorb the verified 026 closeout facts, removed stale residual claims about memory_views fail-open and legacy websocket business-state vocabulary, and restated that 024 project-wrapper host uniformity remains a separate retained item rather than part of the single-episode cleanup stream

Post-closeout review found that 026 Phase 3 had been closed too early: execution_contract is still not the sole video_composer input authority because active context projection continues to infer compose intent from helper state. Rolled governance back accordingly by returning Phase 3 to in_progress, returning Phase 4 to pending, and downgrading the deviation inventory to an interim non-overstated state until the composer contract-authority residual is actually fixed

Completed the repaired 026 Phase 3 composer contract-authority alignment: active orchestration now builds execution_contract before composer context assembly, ContextContractAssembler threads that contract into build_video_composer_context(), helper-side compose inference and requests-based active/integration paths are removed, and validation passed with changed-file py_compile, 17 focused unit passes plus 2 integration passes, and inference-removal grep. Phase 4 remains pending user confirmation

Completed 026 Phase 4 governance refresh: the deviation inventory now reflects the repaired Phase 3 facts without overstating an active composer-authority residual, 024 remains explicitly isolated as the retained project-wrapper host-uniformity item, PLAN_INDEX.json revalidated successfully, and the plan moved to awaiting_user_confirmation rather than completed so final lifecycle closeout still requires explicit user acceptance

User accepted the full 026 result set after Phase 4 closeout, so the managed lifecycle is now marked completed; confirmed_by_user is true and no new implementation or governance scope was added during final closeout

Archived 026 on user request after completion: moved the plan and validation ledger from docs/plans/active/ to docs/plans/archive/2026-03/, updated file_path and archived_at in PLAN_INDEX.json, and left 024 as the only active retained follow-up in this area

plans.title: Project-level read-model persistence and host-neutral job substrate convergence

notes.text: Created a managed successor after 024 C1/C2 proved that planning-host migration is blocked by a larger project-level substrate problem: project read-model visibility is process-local and queue dispatch is still semantically keyed by GenerationMode

Froze the new successor boundary to project-level multi-episode wrapper concerns only: shared project-state/read-model persistence, host-neutral job_kind/handler_key dispatch, and planning-host migration, without reopening single-episode MAS control-plane work
